title: Sequences in COPY FROM <database>TO <otherDB> causes Dependency Error
description:
### What happens?

> ./duckdb original.db
v1.1.2 f680b7d08f
Enter ".help" for usage hints.
D create sequence seq start 1;
D create table tbl (id int default nextval('seq'));
D attach 'backup.db';
D copy from database original to backup;
Dependency Error: Error adding dependency for object "tbl" - dependency "seq" is in catalog "original", which does not match the catalog "backup".
Cross catalog dependencies are not supported.

### To Reproduce

```bash
./duckdb original.db
```
```sql
create sequence seq start 1;
create table tbl (id int default nextval('seq'));
attach 'backup.db';
copy from database original to backup;
```
```console
Dependency Error: Error adding dependency for object "tbl" - dependency "seq" is in catalog "original", which does not match the catalog "backup".
Cross catalog dependencies are not supported.
```
### OS:

Windows 10

### DuckDB Version:

1.1.2

### DuckDB Client:

CLI

### Hardware:

_No response_

### Full Name:

johndapps

bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE tbl_1 (id INT DEFAULT (SELECT MAX(id) + 1 FROM seq_start_1), name VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL);
CREATE TABLE tbl_2 (id INT, value FLOAT CHECK(value >= 0));
INSERT INTO tbl_2 (id, value) VALUES (1, NULL), (2, 3.14);
key_bug_features: Cross catalog dependencies not supported in COPY operation
